prompt: Which Obsidian plugins can effectively replicate Notion's multi-view database functionality (including Table, Kanban, Calendar, and List views)? Please provide a detailed comparison of the strengths and weaknesses of these plugins.
article:
### What “Notion multi‑view database” translates to in Obsidian (assumptions)
To “replicate Notion’s multi‑view database,” you typically need **one canonical dataset** (items = notes/rows, fields = properties) and **multiple synchronized views** over that same dataset:

- **Table** (sortable/filterable; ideally editable inline)
- **List** (often a compact “table-lite”)
- **Kanban / Board** (group by a status-like property; drag/drop should update the underlying property)
- **Calendar** (items placed by a date or start/end date; ideally drag-to-reschedule writes back)
- Plus practicalities: **filtering, sorting, grouping, creation**, and **round‑trip edits** back to the underlying notes’ metadata.

In Obsidian (Feb 2026), the solutions fall into two main “database paradigms”:
1. **Properties-first** (YAML/Properties panel) → **Obsidian Bases** as the core database layer, extended by community view types. Bases stores everything in Markdown note properties and `.base` config files [[1]](https://help.obsidian.md/bases).
2. **Plugin-managed “context database” layer** → **make.md contexts**, which provide their own database/view system (still “from your notes,” but with a stronger plugin layer) [[85]](https://www.make.md/docs/Getting%20Started/Lists%20and%20Databases)[[38]](https://www.make.md/docs/Getting%20Started/Lists%20and%20Databases/Context%20Views).

A third, historically excellent match—**Obsidian Projects**—is **discontinued/archived** and no longer in the community list, so it’s “legacy use only” [[74]](https://github.com/marcusolsson/obsidian-projects).

---

## Shortlist: plugins that can *actually* cover Table + List + Kanban + Calendar as views

### 1) **Obsidian Bases (core) + community view plugins** (closest “native Obsidian” path)
- **Core Bases** provides: **Table, List, Cards (gallery-ish), Map** [[1]](https://help.obsidian.md/bases)[[2]](https://help.obsidian.md/bases/views).  
- **Kanban + Calendar** come from **community layouts registered via the Bases API** (officially supported extension mechanism) [[78]](https://docs.obsidian.md/plugins/guides/bases-view).

This stack is currently the most “Notion-like database” approach **while keeping data in plain Markdown properties** [[1]](https://help.obsidian.md/bases).

### 2) **make.md** (most “Notion-like” all-in-one multi-view experience)
- make.md contexts explicitly support **list, table, board (kanban), calendar** views for the same collection (“context”) [[85]](https://www.make.md/docs/Getting%20Started/Lists%20and%20Databases)[[38]](https://www.make.md/docs/Getting%20Started/Lists%20and%20Databases/Context%20Views).
- Calendar supports **month/week/day**, drag/drop scheduling, and a repeat mechanism via a repeat property [[38]](https://www.make.md/docs/Getting%20Started/Lists%20and%20Databases/Context%20Views).
- Actively maintained with releases as of Jan 2026 [[86]](https://github.com/Make-md/makemd)[[87]](https://github.com/Make-md/makemd/releases).

### 3) **Obsidian Projects** (multi-view, but discontinued)
- Provided **Table, Board, Calendar, Gallery** views on the same notes [[18]](https://marcusolsson.dev/announcing-obsidian-projects/).
- **No explicit List view** (you can approximate with table/gallery).
- Discontinued and removed from the community plugin list; BRAT install required [[74]](https://github.com/marcusolsson/obsidian-projects).

---

# Deep comparison (strengths/weaknesses)

## A. **Obsidian Bases (Core plugin)** — the foundation for a “Notion DB” in Obsidian

### What it covers (out of the box)
**Views supported (core):**
- **Table** [[1]](https://help.obsidian.md/bases)[[4]](https://help.obsidian.md/bases/views/table)
- **List** [[1]](https://help.obsidian.md/bases)[[6]](https://help.obsidian.md/bases/views/list)
- **Cards** (gallery-style) [[1]](https://help.obsidian.md/bases)[[5]](https://help.obsidian.md/bases/views/cards)
- **Map** (requires Maps plugin) [[2]](https://help.obsidian.md/bases/views)[[7]](https://help.obsidian.md/bases/views/map)

**Multi-view per database:** A single base can contain **several views**, each with its own configuration [[2]](https://help.obsidian.md/bases/views).

**Data model & portability (major strength):**
- Data stays in **local Markdown files + their properties** [[1]](https://help.obsidian.md/bases).
- Base definitions can be saved as `.base` files or embedded in Markdown [[1]](https://help.obsidian.md/bases)[[2]](https://help.obsidian.md/bases/views).

### Filtering / sorting / grouping
Bases has a real database-like toolbar:
- **Filter**: property/operator/value, filter groups (and/or/not), plus an “advanced filter editor” for raw syntax [[2]](https://help.obsidian.md/bases/views).
- **Sort**: multiple properties, ordered priority [[2]](https://help.obsidian.md/bases/views).
- **Group**: grouping by property exists but **currently only one group-by property** is supported [[2]](https://help.obsidian.md/bases/views). (This matters for complex Kanban-like swimlanes or nested groupings.)

### Inline editing
Bases is explicitly designed so you can **view and edit** properties in the base view [[1]](https://help.obsidian.md/bases). The Table view docs include commands like **undo/redo changes to properties**, which strongly implies in-table property editing as a first-class workflow [[4]](https://help.obsidian.md/bases/views/table).

### Strengths vs Notion
**Strengths**
- “Database” is not a separate silo: everything stays as normal notes + properties [[1]](https://help.obsidian.md/bases).
- Multiple views (Table/List/Cards/Map) are native and embeddable [[2]](https://help.obsidian.md/bases/views).
- Filtering/sorting/grouping/export are built into the view UI [[2]](https://help.obsidian.md/bases/views).

**Weaknesses / gaps (as of Feb 2026)**
- Core Bases does **not** (yet) ship with a **Kanban view** or a **Calendar view** as built-in layouts; those are on the roadmap as planned items [[3]](https://obsidian.md/roadmap/).
- If your vault uses Dataview-style inline fields heavily, migration to frontmatter/properties may be needed for Bases workflows (community discussion commonly frames this as an issue) [[9]](https://forum.obsidian.md/t/transfer-inline-properties-to-front-matter/104577).

**Bottom line:** Bases is the most robust “native Obsidian database layer,” but you need community view plugins to hit the full Notion view set (Kanban + Calendar).

---

## B. **Bases + Kanban view plugin options** (Board view)

### 1) **Bases Kanban (ewerx/obsidian-bases-kanban)**
**What it does:** Adds a Kanban layout to Bases.  
**Key Notion-like behavior:** Dragging a card between columns **updates frontmatter** of the underlying note (writes back the groupBy property) [[29]](https://github.com/ewerx/obsidian-bases-kanban). It also supports reordering and can update a sort property if configured [[29]](https://github.com/ewerx/obsidian-bases-kanban).

**Strengths**
- True multi-view database: it’s a **layout inside Bases**, so the same dataset can also be Table/List/Cards [[2]](https://help.obsidian.md/bases/views)[[29]](https://github.com/ewerx/obsidian-bases-kanban).
- Explicit write-back model: drag/drop changes persist as property changes in YAML/frontmatter [[29]](https://github.com/ewerx/obsidian-bases-kanban).

**Weaknesses**
- UX edge case reported by users: handling **empty columns** can be imperfect (feedback that column order is in the `.base` config, but empty columns may not show, forcing recreation) [[28]](https://forum.obsidian.md/t/bases-kanban-plugin/109179).
- Requires Obsidian 1.10.6+ [[29]](https://github.com/ewerx/obsidian-bases-kanban).

### 2) **Obsidian Bases Views (AlexandrBukhtatyy/obsidian-bases-views)**
This plugin is broader than “Kanban only”—it adds **Board (Kanban), Calendar, and Gantt** view types to Bases [[82]](https://github.com/AlexandrBukhtatyy/obsidian-bases-views).

**Notion-like strengths (Board)**
- Board view supports drag/drop and grouping by properties (as described) [[82]](https://github.com/AlexandrBukhtatyy/obsidian-bases-views).
- Explicitly states: **“All changes persist to YAML frontmatter”** [[82]](https://github.com/AlexandrBukhtatyy/obsidian-bases-views)—this is the most direct statement of Notion-like two-way persistence.

**Weaknesses / risks**
- It’s a community extension depending on the relatively new Bases API (requires Obsidian 1.10.0+ per the README) [[82]](https://github.com/AlexandrBukhtatyy/obsidian-bases-views).
- Community-plugin review/acceptance status is not clearly established by the captured sources; release notes mention “community plugins PR review” feedback, but that doesn’t guarantee long-term stability/support [[83]](https://github.com/AlexandrBukhtatyy/obsidian-bases-views/releases).
- You’re adding a fairly complex UI layer (board+calendar+gantt) maintained by a third party.

---

## C. **Bases + Calendar view plugin options** (Calendar view)

### 1) **Obsidian Calendar Bases (edrickleong/obsidian-calendar-bases)**
**What it does:** Adds a calendar layout to Bases using FullCalendar [[79]](https://github.com/edrickleong/obsidian-calendar-bases).  
**How items become events:** Configure a **start date property** (must be a valid date string; “any JavaScript-parseable date format”), and optionally an end date property for multi-day events [[79]](https://github.com/edrickleong/obsidian-calendar-bases).

**Strengths**
- True “database calendar view” over your notes, because it is a **Bases layout** [[79]](https://github.com/edrickleong/obsidian-calendar-bases).
- Supports start/end date mapping (multi-day events) [[79]](https://github.com/edrickleong/obsidian-calendar-bases).

**Weaknesses**
- Distribution/availability caveat: README states it was “not yet available under Community Plugins” (under review) at the time of that doc snapshot [[79]](https://github.com/edrickleong/obsidian-calendar-bases).
- The README (at least historically) tied requirements to early access Obsidian 1.10.0+ / Catalyst, which may or may not still be true by Feb 2026 in your installation context [[79]](https://github.com/edrickleong/obsidian-calendar-bases). (The broader point: it depends on Bases API maturity and Obsidian version.)

### 2) **Obsidian Bases Views (again)**
As above, this plugin also claims a Calendar view with:
- Month/Week/Day modes
- Multi-day events
- Drag-and-drop rescheduling and resizing
- Persistence to YAML frontmatter [[82]](https://github.com/AlexandrBukhtatyy/obsidian-bases-views)

If those claims hold in your environment, this is currently the most “Notion-calendar-like” Bases extension based on the provided sources [[82]](https://github.com/AlexandrBukhtatyy/obsidian-bases-views).

---

## D. **make.md** — strongest “Notion-like multi-view database” in one plugin

make.md’s docs explicitly position “contexts” as databases: a context defines properties and views for a collection of notes (folder/tag/space), and you can visualize notes as **table, calendar, list, kanban board** and save filtered views [[85]](https://www.make.md/docs/Getting%20Started/Lists%20and%20Databases).

### View coverage (complete match)
- **List view** (default context view) [[38]](https://www.make.md/docs/Getting%20Started/Lists%20and%20Databases/Context%20Views)
- **Table view** [[38]](https://www.make.md/docs/Getting%20Started/Lists%20and%20Databases/Context%20Views)
- **Board / Kanban** (created via Card View + group-by property) [[38]](https://www.make.md/docs/Getting%20Started/Lists%20and%20Databases/Context%20Views)
- **Calendar**: month/week/day [[38]](https://www.make.md/docs/Getting%20Started/Lists%20and%20Databases/Context%20Views)

### Calendar depth (where make.md is notably strong)
The calendar docs describe interaction patterns that are very Notion-like:
- Drag/drop a path into month/day to “pin” an item and set time [[38]](https://www.make.md/docs/Getting%20Started/Lists%20and%20Databases/Context%20Views)
- Click-drag to create events (day/week), double-click to create (month) [[38]](https://www.make.md/docs/Getting%20Started/Lists%20and%20Databases/Context%20Views)
- Drag events and resize to change time duration [[38]](https://www.make.md/docs/Getting%20Started/Lists%20and%20Databases/Context%20Views)
- Repeating events via a repeat property (object type) [[38]](https://www.make.md/docs/Getting%20Started/Lists%20and%20Databases/Context%20Views)

### Data model & portability (mixed)
- Docs clearly say it manages “data inside your notes through databases” [[85]](https://www.make.md/docs/Getting%20Started/Lists%20and%20Databases) and that contexts define properties for collections of notes [[85]](https://www.make.md/docs/Getting%20Started/Lists%20and%20Databases).
- However, the captured make.md docs/README do **not** explicitly spell out “all fields are stored as YAML frontmatter” the way Bases does [[1]](https://help.obsidian.md/bases) or the Bases Views plugin claims [[82]](https://github.com/AlexandrBukhtatyy/obsidian-bases-views). So while make.md is very capable, there’s more “plugin layer” involved and you should verify how/where it persists specific context metadata in your vault.

### Maintenance signal (strong)
- GitHub shows active development: release **1.3.4 (Jan 26, 2026)** [[86]](https://github.com/Make-md/makemd), with fixes including calendar week-view drag boundary crashes [[87]](https://github.com/Make-md/makemd/releases).

### Strengths vs Notion
**Strengths**
- Most complete out-of-the-box multi-view set (List/Table/Board/Calendar) for the same dataset [[85]](https://www.make.md/docs/Getting%20Started/Lists%20and%20Databases)[[38]](https://www.make.md/docs/Getting%20Started/Lists%20and%20Databases/Context%20Views).
- Calendar interactions (drag/resize/repeat) are documented clearly [[38]](https://www.make.md/docs/Getting%20Started/Lists%20and%20Databases/Context%20Views).
- Also advertises “Two-way Relations, Formulas and Rollups” (Notion-like advanced database features) [[86]](https://github.com/Make-md/makemd).

**Weaknesses**
- Heavier dependence on a single community plugin for core workflow; if you ever remove it, you may need to “unwind” workflows (this is a general risk; not explicitly documented, but implied by make.md being a comprehensive layer).
- Because it’s a feature-rich UI layer, expect more surface area for regressions (example: user-reported plugin load issue after an update) [[89]](https://github.com/Make-md/makemd/issues/462). (Not a condemnation—just a practical risk profile.)

---

## E. **Obsidian Projects (legacy)** — formerly the closest “Notion database clone,” now discontinued
Projects was explicitly designed to give Notion-like multi-view project databases:
- Table, Board, Calendar, Gallery over the same notes [[18]](https://marcusolsson.dev/announcing-obsidian-projects/)
- Table view supports editing properties directly [[18]](https://marcusolsson.dev/announcing-obsidian-projects/)
- Calendar view maps notes with a date property to events and can create new notes by interacting with calendar days [[18]](https://marcusolsson.dev/announcing-obsidian-projects/)

**Critical weakness (Feb 2026 reality): maintenance/availability**
- Discontinued as of May 2025 and removed from community list; BRAT required [[74]](https://github.com/marcusolsson/obsidian-projects).

**Functional limitation**
- If you define a project via a Dataview query rather than a folder, the project becomes **read-only** (no edit/create) [[18]](https://marcusolsson.dev/announcing-obsidian-projects/).

**Bottom line:** If you already run it and accept risk, it still matches the “Notion views” idea well; but for new builds, Bases or make.md are safer.

---

# Plugins that help but don’t fully replicate Notion *multi-view databases* by themselves (still useful)

## 1) **DB Folder (Database Folder)** — Notion-like *tables* with inline editing (but not a full multi-view system)
DB Folder explicitly positions itself as Notion-like databases and supports inline editing:
- “Create Notion’s like databases” [[10]](https://rafaelgb.github.io/obsidian-db-folder/)
- Uses Dataview search engine and lets you “edit the fields directly from the table” without opening the note [[10]](https://rafaelgb.github.io/obsidian-db-folder/)
- Edits are saved into the target note [[16]](https://github.com/RafaelGB/obsidian-db-folder)

It supports many table operations (columns add/hide/reorder/resize; drag-drop reorder for columns) [[76]](https://rafaelgb.github.io/obsidian-db-folder/features/Columns/) and has a **“calendar” column input type** (a date editor), referenced in docs/changelog [[75]](https://rafaelgb.github.io/obsidian-db-folder/about/)[[17]](https://rafaelgb.github.io/obsidian-db-folder/changelog/)—but that is **not** the same as a Notion Calendar *view*.

**Major weakness (Feb 2026): archived**
- Repository archived July 28, 2025 [[16]](https://github.com/RafaelGB/obsidian-db-folder).

## 2) **Dataview** — can render Table/List/Calendar outputs, but is mostly read-only for metadata
Dataview supports:
- **TABLE** and **LIST** queries [[65]](https://blacksmithgu.github.io/obsidian-dataview/queries/query-types/)
- **CALENDAR** query, but it renders a month calendar with **dots** and requires a valid date field; sort/group don’t affect calendar rendering [[65]](https://blacksmithgu.github.io/obsidian-dataview/queries/query-types/)

Dataview generally doesn’t provide Notion-like inline editing of arbitrary fields; it’s commonly described as view-only for metadata (outside of task checkbox interactions) [[11]](https://obsidian.rocks/editing-dataview-tables-with-the-metadata-menu-plugin/). Dataview task queries are interactive for checking tasks, writing back to the source file [[65]](https://blacksmithgu.github.io/obsidian-dataview/queries/query-types/).

### Dataview + editing add-ons
If you want “Notion-like editing,” you often combine:
- **Metadata Menu**, which can modify frontmatter or inline fields and can be used “within dataview tables” [[20]](https://github.com/mdelobelle/metadatamenu). There are established patterns rendering Metadata Menu field controls inside DataviewJS tables for inline edits [[11]](https://obsidian.rocks/editing-dataview-tables-with-the-metadata-menu-plugin/).
- **inlineDB (Dataview custom view)**, which provides an editable table for Dataview queries but has limitations (TABLE-only; limited editable types; relies on MetaEdit) [[12]](https://github.com/vanadium23/obsidian-dataview-inlinedb).
- **Meta Bind**, which creates input controls bound to frontmatter properties (great for interactive note UIs, but not a database view system) [[21]](https://www.moritzjung.dev/obsidian-meta-bind-plugin-docs/)[[22]](https://www.moritzjung.dev/obsidian-meta-bind-plugin-docs/guides/inputfields/).

This stack can approximate a Notion database, but it’s more “build your own database UI.”

## 3) **Obsidian Kanban (mgmeyers)** — great board UX, but not a multi-view database
This plugin creates markdown-backed Kanban boards [[39]](https://github.com/mgmeyers/obsidian-kanban) and integrates with the Tasks plugin (auto-suggest + display task dates in card footer) [[43]](https://publish.obsidian.md/tasks/Other+Plugins/Kanban+Plugin). But:
- It’s looking for new maintainers [[39]](https://github.com/mgmeyers/obsidian-kanban)[[40]](https://github.com/mgmeyers/obsidian-kanban/wiki).
- It can require duplicating due-date metadata between linked note metadata and Kanban’s own card date behaviors (feature request indicates limited linkage) [[41]](https://github.com/mgmeyers/obsidian-kanban/issues/401).
- It does not inherently give you synchronized Table/List/Calendar views over the same dataset (you can build those separately, but it’s not a unified multi-view DB system).

## 4) Task-board aggregators (CardBoard / Task List Kanban / Task Board)
These are strong if your “database rows” are **tasks** distributed across notes, but they’re not Notion-like databases for arbitrary notes with rich properties and multiple synchronized views:
- **CardBoard** aggregates tasks and supports date/tag columns; understands due date formats used by Dataview and Tasks [[47]](https://github.com/roovo/obsidian-card-board) but has reported drag/drop limitations and mobile drag issues in some periods [[48]](https://forum.obsidian.md/t/plugin-cardboard-a-kanban-for-your-markdown-tasks/28314).
- **Task List Kanban** auto-imports tasks and supports drag/drop with two-way sync back to files [[52]](https://github.com/chrskerr/task-list-kanban) (excellent Kanban, not multi-view DB).
- **Task Board** scans tasks vault-wide and supports real-time editing; drag-and-drop was listed as upcoming in its README snapshot [[49]](https://github.com/tu2-atmanand/Task-Board).

---

# Practical recommendations (closest-to-Notion configurations)

## If you want the **most Notion-like “multi-view database” UX** with minimal assembly
**Choose: make.md**
- It explicitly provides list/table/board/calendar views on the same dataset [[85]](https://www.make.md/docs/Getting%20Started/Lists%20and%20Databases)[[38]](https://www.make.md/docs/Getting%20Started/Lists%20and%20Databases/Context%20Views).
- Calendar supports drag/drop and resize + repeat [[38]](https://www.make.md/docs/Getting%20Started/Lists%20and%20Databases/Context%20Views).
- Active maintenance into Jan 2026 [[86]](https://github.com/Make-md/makemd)[[87]](https://github.com/Make-md/makemd/releases).

## If you want the **most Obsidian-native, Markdown-first** database where everything is plain properties
**Choose: Bases + community views**
- Core Bases: strong table/list/cards, filtering/sorting/grouping, edit properties, export, multiple views per base [[1]](https://help.obsidian.md/bases)[[2]](https://help.obsidian.md/bases/views)[[4]](https://help.obsidian.md/bases/views/table)[[6]](https://help.obsidian.md/bases/views/list).
- Add:
  - **Bases Kanban** for board drag/drop writing to frontmatter [[29]](https://github.com/ewerx/obsidian-bases-kanban)
  - A Bases calendar view plugin such as **Obsidian Calendar Bases** [[79]](https://github.com/edrickleong/obsidian-calendar-bases) or **Obsidian Bases Views** (board+calendar+gantt; claims YAML persistence and drag/resize) [[82]](https://github.com/AlexandrBukhtatyy/obsidian-bases-views)

This is the best path if you care about long-term vault portability because Bases stores data in note properties [[1]](https://help.obsidian.md/bases).

## If you already use it and accept risk
**Obsidian Projects** is conceptually very close to Notion (Table/Board/Calendar/Gallery) [[18]](https://marcusolsson.dev/announcing-obsidian-projects/), but it’s discontinued/removed from the directory [[74]](https://github.com/marcusolsson/obsidian-projects).

---

# Summary table (high-level)

| Option | Table | List | Kanban | Calendar | Writes back to note properties? | Notes |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---|
| **Bases (core)** | Yes [[4]](https://help.obsidian.md/bases/views/table) | Yes [[6]](https://help.obsidian.md/bases/views/list) | Not core (planned) [[3]](https://obsidian.md/roadmap/) | Not core (planned) [[3]](https://obsidian.md/roadmap/) | Yes (properties editing is core intent) [[1]](https://help.obsidian.md/bases)[[4]](https://help.obsidian.md/bases/views/table) | Best “native DB layer”; extend via Bases API [[78]](https://docs.obsidian.md/plugins/guides/bases-view) |
| **Bases + Bases Kanban** | Yes [[4]](https://help.obsidian.md/bases/views/table) | Yes [[6]](https://help.obsidian.md/bases/views/list) | Yes [[29]](https://github.com/ewerx/obsidian-bases-kanban) | Not included | Yes (drag updates frontmatter) [[29]](https://github.com/ewerx/obsidian-bases-kanban) | True multi-view over same dataset |
| **Bases + Calendar Bases** | Yes [[4]](https://help.obsidian.md/bases/views/table) | Yes [[6]](https://help.obsidian.md/bases/views/list) | Not included | Yes [[79]](https://github.com/edrickleong/obsidian-calendar-bases) | (Implied) uses properties; date mapping via properties [[79]](https://github.com/edrickleong/obsidian-calendar-bases) | Availability may vary; check distribution state [[79]](https://github.com/edrickleong/obsidian-calendar-bases) |
| **Bases + Bases Views plugin** | Yes (via Bases) [[1]](https://help.obsidian.md/bases) | Yes (via Bases) [[1]](https://help.obsidian.md/bases) | Yes [[82]](https://github.com/AlexandrBukhtatyy/obsidian-bases-views) | Yes [[82]](https://github.com/AlexandrBukhtatyy/obsidian-bases-views) | Explicitly yes (YAML persistence claim) [[82]](https://github.com/AlexandrBukhtatyy/obsidian-bases-views) | Adds board+calendar+gantt; community dependency |
| **make.md** | Yes [[38]](https://www.make.md/docs/Getting%20Started/Lists%20and%20Databases/Context%20Views) | Yes [[38]](https://www.make.md/docs/Getting%20Started/Lists%20and%20Databases/Context%20Views) | Yes [[38]](https://www.make.md/docs/Getting%20Started/Lists%20and%20Databases/Context%20Views) | Yes [[38]](https://www.make.md/docs/Getting%20Started/Lists%20and%20Databases/Context%20Views) | Edits are part of “edit your data” flows [[38]](https://www.make.md/docs/Getting%20Started/Lists%20and%20Databases/Context%20Views)[[85]](https://www.make.md/docs/Getting%20Started/Lists%20and%20Databases) | Most Notion-like UX; verify storage details for your needs |
| **Projects (legacy)** | Yes [[18]](https://marcusolsson.dev/announcing-obsidian-projects/) | Not explicit | Yes [[18]](https://marcusolsson.dev/announcing-obsidian-projects/) | Yes [[18]](https://marcusolsson.dev/announcing-obsidian-projects/) | Yes (folder-based projects), but Dataview projects read-only [[18]](https://marcusolsson.dev/announcing-obsidian-projects/) | Discontinued/removed [[74]](https://github.com/marcusolsson/obsidian-projects) |

---

## Bottom line
- **Best “Notion-style multi-view database” today:** **make.md** (complete view set; rich calendar interactions; actively maintained) [[85]](https://www.make.md/docs/Getting%20Started/Lists%20and%20Databases)[[38]](https://www.make.md/docs/Getting%20Started/Lists%20and%20Databases/Context%20Views)[[87]](https://github.com/Make-md/makemd/releases).
- **Best “Notion databases, but fully Markdown-native and increasingly first-party”:** **Obsidian Bases** as the database layer [[1]](https://help.obsidian.md/bases)[[2]](https://help.obsidian.md/bases/views), extended with **Bases Kanban** [[29]](https://github.com/ewerx/obsidian-bases-kanban) and a **Bases Calendar** plugin such as Calendar Bases [[79]](https://github.com/edrickleong/obsidian-calendar-bases) or Bases Views [[82]](https://github.com/AlexandrBukhtatyy/obsidian-bases-views). This gives you the closest equivalent to Notion databases while keeping your vault maximally portable (plain notes + properties) [[1]](https://help.obsidian.md/bases).
